perturbation.original_text: A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.changed_text: A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than ten (10) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the renewal notice period from 30 days to 10 days may conflict with implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code § 1203 establishes an obligation of good faith in the performance and enforcement of contracts. Reducing the notice period to 10 days before automatic renewal, while not explicitly illegal, might be deemed a violation of good faith if it unreasonably prejudices the other party's ability to evaluate and decide on renewal, thereby undermining their contractual expectations and benefits.

GENERAL PROVISIONS [1101 - 1310] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 2. General Definitions and Principles of Interpretation [1201 - 1206] ( Chapter 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 1203. (a) Whether a transaction in the form of a lease creates a lease or security interest is determined by the facts of each case. (b) A transaction in the form of a lease creates a security interest if the consideration that the lessee is to pay the lessor for the right to possession and use of the goods is an obligation for the term of the lease and is not subject to termination by the lessee, and: (1) the original term of the lease is equal to or greater than the remaining economic life of the goods; (2) the lessee is bound to renew the lease for the remaining economic life of the goods or is bound to become the owner of the goods; (3) the lessee has an option to renew the lease for the remaining economic life of the goods for no additional consideration or for nominal additional consideration upon compliance with the lease agreement; or (4) the lessee has an option to become the owner of the goods for no additional consideration or for nominal additional consideration upon compliance with the lease agreement. perturbation.justification: The change from 30 days to 10 days for renewal notice potentially violates the implied covenant of good faith, as explained in the Perturbation and Law Explanations. While California Commercial Code § 1203, provided in Scraped Law Snippet 1, primarily addresses lease agreements and security interests, the Law Explanation suggests the good faith obligation it establishes could extend to renewal notice periods, especially if shortening the period unreasonably prejudices the other party's ability to evaluate the renewal. The change directly impacts a time-sensitive obligation, fitting the 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' definition, where altering a deadline potentially renders the contractual term unenforceable due to conflicting with implied legal standards of fairness. The shorter notice period weakens the other party's opportunity to make an informed decision. This makes the change a contradiction.
